# kemp_cipher_set (Resource)

Manages a custom TLS cipher set on a Kemp LoadMaster.

A cipher set is a named, ordered list of TLS cipher strings. Once defined, a cipher set can be assigned to virtual services via the LoadMaster WUI (or via `modvs` with the `CipherSet` parameter). Built-in sets (`Default`, `BestPractices`, `FIPS`, etc.) can be read as data sources but cannot be managed with this resource.

**Cipher order matters** — LoadMaster presents ciphers to clients in the order listed. Put the strongest ciphers first.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "kemp_cipher_set" "modern_tls" {
  name = "ModernTLS"
  ciphers = [
    "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384",
    "ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384",
    "ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305",
    "ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305",
  ]
}

# Assign the cipher set to a virtual service
resource "kemp_virtual_service" "example" {
  address    = "10.0.0.1"
  port       = "443"
  protocol   = "tcp"
  cipher_set = kemp_cipher_set.modern_tls.name
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ciphers` (List of String) **Required.** Ordered list of OpenSSL cipher strings. LoadMaster joins them with `:` on the wire. Example: `["ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384", "ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384"]`.
- `name` (String) **Required.** Unique name for the cipher set. Forces replacement if changed.
